ddls:
CREATE TABLE "requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the request was created */,
  "requester_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the request */,
  "addressee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the request is addressed */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the request was completed or closed */,
  "stages" INTEGER /* Number of stages the request has gone through */,
  "result" INTEGER /* Result of the request (e.g., approved, denied) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the request in days */,
  "activity_start_date" TEXT /* Date the request activity started */,
  "request_type" TEXT /* Type of request (e.g., leave, expense, project) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the request (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_addressee_id FOREIGN KEY ("addressee_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_requester_id FOREIGN KEY ("requester_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the user belongs to */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., employee, manager, admin) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department's responsibilities */,
  "head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_stages" (
  "stage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stage */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request the stage is part of */,
  "stage_name" TEXT /* Name of the stage (e.g., submission, approval, completion) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the stage started */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the stage ended */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the stage (e.g., in progress, completed) */,
  "stage_description" TEXT /* Description of the stage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_stages_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request the history entry is for */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date the change was made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., status update, comment) */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Details of the change (e.g., new status, comment text) */,
  "changed_by_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_history_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_history_changed_by_id FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_types" (
  "request_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request type */,
  "request_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the request type (e.g., leave, expense, project) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the request type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_priorities" (
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each priority */,
  "priority_name" TEXT /* Name of the priority (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the priority */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("priority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_stage_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., in progress, completed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)
